solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public long minEnd(int n, int x) {
        long res = (long) x;
        long bit = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
            long next = res;
            while ((next & x) != x) {
                next += bit;
            }
            res = next;
            bit <<= 1;
        }
        return res;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm iteratively builds the array by finding the next number greater than the previous one that maintains the bitwise AND result as x. It uses a bit-shifting approach to incrementally find the next valid number. The main strength is its simplicity and clarity, but the linear time complexity may be a limitation for very large n.
